Tool Usage: MerMED-FM is a state-of-the-art multimodal, multi-specialty foundation model trained using self-supervised learning and a memory module on 3.3 million medical images from over ten specialties and seven modalities, enabling robust medical imaging interpretation across diverse medical disciplines including radiology, pathology, ophthalmology, and dermatology.
Architecture: Self-supervised learning architecture with teacher-student iterative training system and memory-based module. Trained on 3.3 million medical images across multiple modalities within a single architecture, enabling broad generalization and cross-specialty knowledge transfer.
Description: MerMED-FM was trained on 3.3 million medical images from over ten specialties (eye, lung, adrenal, spleen, kidney, bladder, prostate, liver, gallbladder, pancreas, colon, ovarian, uterine, bone, thyroid, skin, vessels) and seven modalities, including CT, chest X-rays, ultrasound, pathology patches, color fundus photography, OCT and dermatology images. The model introduces a teacher-student iterative training system and a memory-based module to help the model learn more accurately from limited data and stay consistent across tasks.
Evaluation: 25 publicly available datasets; Multiple disease types and imaging modalities; Mean AUROC of 0.935 (vs 0.919 for BiomedCLIP, 0.933 for DINO); Sensitivity 78.3%, Specificity 91.8%; AUROCs by modality: 0.988 (OCT), 0.982 (pathology), 0.951 (US), 0.943 (CT), 0.931 (skin), 0.894 (CFP), 0.858 (CXR); Lung cancer detection on CT: AUROC 0.981; COVID-19 pneumonia on CT: AUROC 0.999; Pneumothorax on CXR: AUROC 0.934; Performance across eye diseases, lung diseases, malignancies, and skin diseases

Tool Usage: Efficient RETFound variant matching performance with 400x less compute (CFP only)
Description: RETFound-Green uses ViT encoder with Token Reconstruction SSL, more efficient than MAE. Encoder-only architecture for retinal imaging. (2024)
Evaluation: Disease detection AUROC; Diabetic retinopathy; Glaucoma; AMD; Systemic disease prediction; Label efficiency

Tool Usage: Foundation model for dermoscopic image analysis supporting melanoma diagnosis, lesion change detection, and longitudinal monitoring with strong label efficiency.
Architecture: ViT-Large Image Encoder + Masked Latent Regressor
Description: PanDerm integrates dermoscopic images into a multimodal dermatology foundation model, enabling early melanoma detection, lesion change analysis, and metastasis risk prediction from sequential imaging.
Evaluation: Melanoma diagnosis, lesion change detection

Tool Usage: Deep learning combining fundus images with clinical risk factors for cardiovascular disease prediction
Description: Hybrid CNN/Transformer architecture for cardiovascular disease prediction using multimodal clinical data. Combines convolutional and attention mechanisms.
Evaluation: ECG classification

Tool Usage: LVM-Med is a large-scale self-supervised vision model for medical imaging that learns representations via second-order graph matching, a novel contrastive learning algorithm that integrates pairwise image similarity and captures structural constraints of feature embeddings, enabling effective fine-tuning across diverse medical imaging tasks.
Architecture: ResNet-50 and Vision Transformer (ViT) networks trained using second-order graph matching self-supervised learning. The approach constructs graphs from transformed image embeddings and solves a graph-matching problem to learn feature representations. Trained on approximately 1.3 million medical images from 55 publicly available datasets covering various organs and modalities (CT, MRI, X-ray, ultrasound, dermoscopy).
Description: LVM-Med is trained on approximately 1.3 million medical images from 55 publicly available datasets covering various body organs and modalities. The model uses a novel self-supervised contrastive learning algorithm based on second-order graph matching formulation that integrates global and local information, captures structural constraints through edge constraints, and can be trained efficiently end-to-end using gradient-estimation techniques.
Evaluation: 15 downstream medical tasks: segmentation (fully fine-tuning and prompt-based), classification (linear and fully fine-tuning), object detection; In-distribution and out-of-distribution settings; Brain Tumor Classification; Diabetic Retinopathy Grading; Performance improvements of 6-7% over vision-language models trained on 1 billion masks while using only ResNet-50

Tool Usage: RETFound is a foundation model for retinal images that learns generalizable representations from unlabelled retinal images using self-supervised learning (masked autoencoder) and provides a basis for label-efficient model adaptation in disease detection tasks including ocular diseases and systemic disorders.
Architecture: Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture pretrained using Masked Autoencoder (MAE) self-supervised learning. Two separate models: one for CFP (904,170 images) and one for OCT (736,442 images), totaling 1.6 million retinal images from MEH-MIDAS and public datasets. Pretrained successively on ImageNet-1k followed by retinal images.
Description: The paper demonstrates that retinal features can encode signals predictive of both ocular and systemic diseases, enabling high-precision diagnosis directly from retinal images. RETFound shows superior label efficiency and generalizability compared to ImageNet-pretrained models. The masked autoencoder approach generally outperforms contrastive SSL approaches (SimCLR, SwAV, DINO, MoCo-v3) in most tasks.
Evaluation: Ocular disease diagnosis: 8 publicly available datasets; Diabetic retinopathy (AUROC 0.943 on APTOS-2019, 0.822 on IDRID, 0.884 on MESSIDOR-2); Glaucoma; Multiclass disease classification; Ocular disease prognosis: 1-year prediction of fellow eye converting to wet-AMD (AUROC 0.862 with CFP, 0.799 with OCT); Systemic disease prediction: 3-year prediction of ischaemic stroke (AUROC 0.754 with CFP), myocardial infarction (AUROC 0.737 with CFP), heart failure (AUROC 0.794 with CFP), Parkinson's disease (AUROC 0.669); Label efficiency: outperforms with only 10% of labelled training data; External validation on UK Biobank

Tool Usage: FFT-based multimodal fusion of ECG signals with fundus photography for CVD prediction (~84% accuracy)
Description: Dual encoder for multimodal ECG-fundus image learning.
Evaluation: ECG classification

Tool Usage: Google's multimodal model supporting fundus/ophthalmology image interpretation with Natural language (CFP only)
Description: MedGemma is based on Google Gemma decoder-only architecture fine-tuned for medical tasks using instruction tuning. (Google, 2024)
Evaluation: ECG classification

Tool Usage: Uses SLICE-3D (3D-TBP lesion crops) and learns representations from lesion images + lesion/patient conNatural language (metadata), framed as a multi-modal contrastive approach. This is ‚Äö√Ñ√∫Natural language‚Äö√Ñ√π in the sense of tokenizable structured clinical conNatural language (often rendered into Natural language prompts)
Architecture: ViT Image Encoder + Tabular Transformer Encoders
Description: Transformer encoder architecture.
Evaluation: SLIMP (Skin Lesion Image Metadata Pre-training) learns rich skin lesion representations by aligning lesion images with structured lesion- and patient-level metadata through a nested contrastive learning scheme, enabling robust lesion phenotyping, metadata-aware classification, and retrieval even under limited annotation or missing metadata.

Tool Usage: This is a "Generalist" medical foundation model trained on 3.3 million images across 7 modalities, including both Pathology patches (H&E) and Color Fundus Photography (CFP).
Architecture: Dual-network teacher-student ViT framework with a dynamic memory module
Description: A multimodal, multi-specialty foundation model trained using self-supervised learning (SSL) to unify the interpretation of diverse imaging types through a shared latent space.
Evaluation: Detection of lung carcinoma and COVID-19 pneumonia on CT scan slices.

Tool Usage: A suite of task-agnostic models. It provides frozen feature representations that generalized to new tasks without intensive fine-tuning.
Description: Scalable pre-training on 105k volumes using 3D adaptations of DINOv2 and depth-aware embeddings.
Evaluation: Low-shot transfer (AUROC); Feature test-retest; Segmentation; Retrieval.

Tool Usage: A universal, text-guided assistant. It performs segmentation via text prompts (e.g., "segment tumor") rather than requiring spatial cues like clicks.
Description: Full-parameter fine-tuning on 33 datasets across 10 modalities to handle medical concepts without spatial prompts.
Evaluation: Zero-shot Dice score; Semantic-driven segmentation; External dataset generalization.

Tool Usage: Uses "geometric context" priors to learn representations. It is used to reduce labeled data dependency for complex tasks like volume registration.
Description: Implicitly encodes geometric relations between organs using volume contrastive learning on 160,000 CT volumes.
Evaluation: 51 tasks: Segmentation, Classification, Registration, Vision-Language.

Tool Usage: Efficient for whole-body segmentation and head CT triage. It enables doctors to cluster anatomical regions and identify structural similarities across scans without manual labels.
Description: Pre-trained on 148,000 scans from Imaging Data Commons using label-agnostic contrastive learning.
Evaluation: Whole-body/tumor segmentation; Head CT triage; Image retrieval.

Tool Usage: A Microsoft embedding model for holistic analysis. It routes scans to specialists and flags abnormalities for review through similarity search.
Description: Pre-trained on 3.7 million image-text pairs using the UniCL objective for task-agnostic medical embeddings.
Evaluation: Image-text/image-image search; Zero-shot classification.

Tool Usage: Facilitates human-in-the-loop workflows. It provides automatic segmentation for 127 classes which can be refined in real-time by point clicks.
Description: Multi-instance training with supervoxel supervision on 11,454 scans to achieve high-accuracy interactive segmentation.
Evaluation: Automatic segmentation (127 classes); Interactive refinement; Zero-shot unseen classes.

Tool Usage: Classification, segmentation, and object detection
Architecture: ResNet-50 or ViT-B/16 backbones with a second-order graph matching SSL formulation
Description: A general-purpose medical vision foundation model pre-trained on 1.3 million multi-modal images using graph-based structural constraints.
Evaluation: Multi-organ and heart structure segmentation, and abdominal organ analysis.

Tool Usage: The largest medical segmentation model series. It serves as a high-performance baseline for scaling AI capabilities in 3D medical imaging.
Description: Empirical scaling of model depth and width (up to 1.4B parameters) using supervised pre-training on TotalSegmentator.
Evaluation: Direct inference (14 datasets); Fine-tuning (3 datasets); TotalSegmentator

Tool Usage: A deep learning framework that reconstructs 3D gene expression signatures from volumetric micro-CT (X-ray) data using convolutional neural networks.
Architecture: 3D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) backbone + Regressor head
Description: X-Pression uses hybrid CNN/Transformer architecture for predicting gene expression from tissue morphology images. (2024)
Evaluation: SARS-CoV-2 vaccine efficacy, viral replication dynamics, and lung pathology (e.g., inflammation-induced atelectasis) in mouse models.

Model Name: ECG foundation model based on a modified Vision Transformer
Tool Usage: ECG signals to predict cardiac information that is normally measured by imaging, including PET, SPECT, and cardiac magnetic resonance (CMR)
Description: Modified ViT encoder treating ECG as image-like signals.
Evaluation: Radiology image classification; ECG classification

Tool Usage: Self-supervised ECG foundation model that adapts HuBERT-style masked prediction with cluster-based pseudo-labels to learn transferable representations from large-scale unlabeled ECG waveforms.
Architecture: Convolutional ECG Feature Extractor + Transformer Encoder
Description: HuBERT-ECG transfers the HuBERT paradigm from speech to ECG by predicting masked ECG segment cluster assignments using a Transformer encoder, enabling label-efficient learning of general-purpose ECG representations that can be fine-tuned across diverse downstream cardiac tasks.
Evaluation: ECG interpretation (multi-label classification); arrhythmia detection; rhythm and morphology classification

Tool Usage: A 2025 paper introducing an "open-weight" model that is specifically validated for predicting heart function (LVEF).
Description: ECG-FM uses Transformer encoder architecture as a foundation model for ECG signals. Self-supervised pretraining on large-scale ECG databases. (2024)
Evaluation: ECG classification; Arrhythmia detection; 12-lead analysis; Transfer learning

Tool Usage: Self-supervised masked autoencoder pretraining on raw ECG signals from digital stethoscopes, followed by fine-tuning for cardiovascular disease detection tasks.
Description: Foundation model trained on ECG time-series using masked autoencoding; learns general-purpose cardiac representations transferable to disease classification tasks such as atrial fibrillation and low ejection fraction detection.
Evaluation: ECG-based disease classification (e.g., AF detection, low EF screening)

Tool Usage: This is the most comprehensive paper (published 2025) using 10 million recordings to create a general-purpose heart foundation model.
Description: ECGFounder uses Transformer encoder architecture trained on 10M+ ECGs with masked signal prediction. Large-scale encoder foundation model. (2024)
Evaluation: Multi-task ECG benchmarks; Arrhythmia; HF prediction; Cross-dataset evaluation

Tool Usage: The model leverages longitudinal electronic health records to perform zero-shot forecasting of future medical events, predicting clinical outcomes without requiring task-specific fine-tuning
Description: This study presents a pipeline that formulates medical concept prediction as a generative modeling task to evaluate the zero-shot capabilities of foundational GPT models on electronic health record data
Evaluation: Downstream task fine-tuning

Tool Usage: UniHPF is a universal healthcare predictive framework, which requires no medical domain knowledge and minimal preprocessing for multiple prediction tasks. Given any time-series EHR
Description: UniHPF uses unified Transformer encoder model for healthcare prediction across multiple tasks. (2024)
Evaluation: 34 clinical tasks in anatomic pathology; ROI classification; Slide-level classification; Segmentation (SegPath); Retrieval

Tool Usage: pretrained on timestamped sequences of events in electronic health records (EHR) and health insurance claims
Description: MOTOR uses Transformer encoder for medical outcomes prediction from EHR data. Foundation encoder for clinical trajectories. (2024)
Evaluation: Task-specific fine-tuning

Tool Usage: adapts the Mamba architecture, a Selective State Space Model (SSM), to EHR data.
Description: EHRMamba uses Mamba state-space model architecture for EHR sequential data. Uses selective state space layers instead of attention. (Yang et al., 2024)
Evaluation: EHR sequential modeling; Diagnosis prediction; State-space model evaluation

Tool Usage: CLMBR-T-base is a structured EHR foundation model that encodes a patient's longitudinal medical history to facilitate downstream clinical prediction tasks and risk stratification. [cite: 20, 21, 39]
Description: CLMBR-T-Base uses Transformer encoder for clinical language model benchmarking. BERT-style encoder on EHR data. (2022)
Evaluation: Downstream task fine-tuning

Tool Usage: incorporate temporal information using a hybrid approach by augmenting the input to BERT using artificial time tokens, incorporating time, age, and concept embeddings
Description: CEHR-BERT uses BERT encoder architecture adapted for EHR concept sequences. Bidirectional Transformer encoder with masked language modeling on medical events. (Pang et al., 2021)
Evaluation: EHR prediction; Hospital readmission; Mortality prediction; Phenotyping

Tool Usage: This model modifies the standard BERT architecture to accommodate the hierarchical nature of medical codes. It employs four distinct embedding layers:
Description: BEHRT adapts BERT architecture for EHR data. Uses Transformer encoder with bidirectional attention for patient trajectory modeling from medical codes. (Li et al., Scientific Reports 2020)
Evaluation: Disease prediction; EHR trajectory modeling; MIMIC/UK Biobank evaluation

Tool Usage: Deep learning (DL)-based predictive models from electronic health records (EHRs) deliver impressive performance in many clinical tasks.
Description: Med-BERT uses BERT-style Transformer encoder for structured EHR data. Pretrained with masked diagnosis prediction on large EHR datasets. (Rasmy et al., npj Digital Medicine 2021)
Evaluation: Downstream task fine-tuning

Tool Usage: The system harnesses both fundus images and electronic health records (EHR) for early and enhanced diabetic retinopathy detection by capturing the evolving dynamics of patient histories over time
Architecture: The temporal multi-modal architecture combines an EfficientNetB5 model pre-trained on the DDR dataset for retinal image processing, and a TabTransformer with 4 attention layers for incorporating categorical and continuous EHR data, fused via multi-layer perceptrons
Description: A dual-model machine learning system comprising a temporal tabular model that relies solely on historical medical records and a deep learning multi-modal model that combines these records with fundus images
Evaluation: Image classification benchmark

Tool Usage: This study introduces MLEDL, a multimodal longitudinal deep learning system that integrates color fundus photographs and clinical Natural language to predict current and future visual fields, enabling efficient functional assessment of glaucoma progression without repeated VF testing.
Architecture: The architecture uses ResNet-50 as the backbone to extract image features
Description: MLEDL is an ensemble of multiple deep learning models including CNNs and Transformers. Multi-model ensemble approach. (2024)
Evaluation: Domain-specific evaluation

Tool Usage: This study develops DeepDR-LLM, an integrated image language system that combines a deep-learning DR screening model (DeepDR-Transformer) with a large language model to deliver PCP-facing diabetes management recommendations, improving referable DR detection, care adherence, and recommendation quality in real-world clinical deployment.
Description: DeepDR-LLM integrates clinical metadata (such as medical history and laboratory tests) with standard or portable fundus images to automatically generate personalized diabetes management plans and evaluate image quality, lesion segmentation, and diabetic retinopathy grades.
Evaluation: Perplexity evaluation; Generation quality assessment

Tool Usage: HONeYBEE uses domain-specific foundation models to encode clinical Natural language and structured EHR, whole-slide pathology images, radiology scans, and molecular profiles, and fuses these multimodal embeddings into unified patient-level representations for downstream oncology tasks such as survival prediction, cancer classification, patient retrieval, and cohort clustering.
Description: HONeYBEE uses multiple domain-specific foundation models to encode clinical text (BioClinicalBERT), EHR (MOTOR), pathology (CONCH), radiology (BioViL), and molecular data. Fuses multimodal embeddings using separate encoders. (npj Digital Medicine 2025)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: A multimodal vision language AI framework that leverages a pretrained large language model to extract EHR information and guide CT-based lung tumor auto-segmentation, reducing false positives and improving accuracy for radiotherapy planning in NSCLC.
Architecture: A pre-trained Large Language Model (GPT 3.5 Turbo) for text extraction with a Retina-UNet3D framework for 3D object detection and segmentation
Description: EXACT-Net integrates a zero-shot large language model for natural language processing of medical reports with a 3D convolutional neural network for image segmentation. By extracting the confirmed tumor location from the text, the framework guides the segmentation network to mask irrelevant structures and eliminate false positive nodules
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: The researchers developed MetaGP, a 32-billion-parameter generative foundation model , by pretraining it on a massive corpus of 8 million electronic health records (EHRs), 5.4 million academic articles, and over 15,000 medical books using 120 NVIDIA A100 GPUs. To address complex clinical needs, they fine-tuned the model with supervised instruction tuning on specific medical QA and EHR datasets while integrating Swin Transformer and ViT-3D vision encoders to enable the generation of multimodal medical reports for 2D chest X-rays and 3D CT scans.
Description: MetaGP uses 32B generative decoder model with Swin Transformer and ViT-3D vision encoders for multimodal medical AI. Primarily decoder-based. (2024)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: This study employs Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to automatically detect pulmonary embolism related abnormalities from CTPA images and integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate structured radiology reports, which are further combined with imaging and clinical data in a multimodal fusion model for outcome (survival) prediction.
Description: Generic Vision-Language Model + LLM architecture. Uses frozen vision encoder (CLIP/ViT) connected to LLM decoder for visual understanding. (Various, 2024)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: This paper evaluates the MEHRT (Multimodal EHR Transformer) by pretraining it on longitudinal structured records, CT scans, and histopathology images, using a multi-stage curriculum strategy to adapt a structured-only backbone into a multimodal model. The authors utilize DINOv2 and GigaPath as modality-specific frozen encoders to tokenize images into visual embeddings, which are then integrated with medical code tokens in an autoregressive Llama-3.2 architecture.
Description: MEHRT (Multimodal EHR Transformer) uses Llama-3.2 backbone with frozen DINOv2 and GigaPath image encoders. Combines LLM decoder with vision encoders for EHR+imaging. (NeurIPS 2025)
Evaluation: Histopathology classification

Tool Usage: An EHR-based foundation model that converts ECG signals and X-ray report features into a unified Natural language-based trajectory.
Architecture: EHR Foundation Model (BERT-style encoder) + 1D ResNet ECG encoder
Description: A framework that integrates text-based representations of unstructured diagnostic reports directly into longitudinal EHR patient trajectories to preserve temporal and clinical context.
Evaluation: Prediction of heart failure, ischemic heart disease, coronary artery disease, and stroke.

Tool Usage: A multimodal AI pipeline that combines a pretrained EHR foundation model with a contrastive ECG image echocardiogram vision-language model to enable targeted, risk-stratified AI-ECG screening for structural heart disease, reducing false positives while improving diagnostic performance.
Description: TARGET-AI uses hybrid Transformer/CNN architecture for targeted medical analysis. Combines convolutional and attention mechanisms. (2024)
Evaluation: ECG classification

Tool Usage: This study presents a joint ECG EHR multimodal fusion framework that integrates ECG images and structured clinical records using an attention-enhanced multi-branch CNN, demonstrating superior performance over ViT and EHR-only baselines for predicting major adverse clinical outcomes after PCI without relying on laboratory or vital sign data.
Description: Hybrid architecture combining CNN with Vision Transformer attention mechanisms. Uses both convolutional and self-attention layers. (Various)
Evaluation: ECG classification

Model Name: All of Us (AoU) EHR-Genomics Foundation Model
Tool Usage: utilizes cross-attention mechanisms to fuse longitudinal patient trajectories with genetic susceptibility profiles.
Description: Transformer encoder for multimodal EHR-genomics integration in the All of Us research program.
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction

Tool Usage: medical Natural language and image comprehension
Description: MedGemma is based on Google Gemma decoder-only architecture fine-tuned for medical tasks using instruction tuning. (Google, 2024)
Evaluation: Histopathology classification

Tool Usage: HONeYBEE uses domain-specific foundation models to encode clinical Natural language and structured EHR, whole-slide pathology images, radiology scans, and molecular profiles, and fuses these multimodal embeddings into unified patient-level representations for downstream oncology tasks such as survival prediction, cancer classification, patient retrieval, and cohort clustering.
Description: HONeYBEE uses multiple domain-specific foundation models to encode clinical text (BioClinicalBERT), EHR (MOTOR), pathology (CONCH), radiology (BioViL), and molecular data. Fuses multimodal embeddings using separate encoders. (npj Digital Medicine 2025)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: This study proposes a self-supervised pretrained multimodal deep neural network that fuses retinal OCT and infrared reflectance imaging with structured clinical data to enable stroke detection and future risk prediction, outperforming unimodal imaging models and existing foundation-model baselines.
Description: Generic multimodal deep learning using hybrid CNN/Transformer architectures for medical data fusion. (Various)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: This study introduces a hybrid neuro-symbolic AI + large language model framework that integrates a domain-specific ophthalmic knowledge graph, multimodal retinal imaging pipelines, ontology-mapped clinical Natural language, and an LLM for evidence-grounded natural-language explanations to deliver accurate, causally interpretable AMD treatment-prognosis predictions.
Description: Hybrid architecture combining neuro-symbolic reasoning with LLM decoder. Integrates symbolic AI with neural language models. (Various)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: This study applies TabNet deep learning and XGBoost to build single-modality and multimodal fusion prediction models that integrate structured EHR data with RNFL OCT features to predict 12-month glaucoma surgery risk, demonstrating superior performance of the TabNet-based EHR+OCT fusion model
Description: TabNet+XGBoost is a hybrid ensemble combining TabNet (attention-based) with XGBoost (gradient boosting). Non-standard Transformer hybrid. (Various)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: HONeYBEE uses domain-specific foundation models to encode clinical Natural language and structured EHR, whole-slide pathology images, radiology scans, and molecular profiles, and fuses these multimodal embeddings into unified patient-level representations for downstream oncology tasks such as survival prediction, cancer classification, patient retrieval, and cohort clustering.
Description: HONeYBEE uses multiple domain-specific foundation models to encode clinical text (BioClinicalBERT), EHR (MOTOR), pathology (CONCH), radiology (BioViL), and molecular data. Fuses multimodal embeddings using separate encoders. (npj Digital Medicine 2025)
Evaluation: Downstream task fine-tuning

Tool Usage: Mixture-of-Multimodal-Agents (MoMA), designed to leverage multiple large language model (LLM) agents for clinical prediction tasks using multimodal EHR data.
Description: MOMA uses Transformer encoder for medical omics analysis. Encoder architecture for multi-omics integration. (2024)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: DRfold2 is a deep learning framework that performs end-to-end ab initio RNA 3D structure prediction directly from a single RNA sequence.
Architecture: Integrates a pre-trained RNA Composite Language Model (RCLM) with 18 RNA Transformer blocks (Evoformer-based) trained via composite likelihood maximization on ~30M RNA sequences. The structure prediction pipeline uses 16 RNA Transformer blocks and a Denoising RNA Structure Module (DRSM) with Invariant Point Attention (IPA), followed by a CSOR (Clustering, Scoring, Optimization, and Refinement) post-processing protocol.
Description: DRfold2 predicts RNA 3D structures from single sequences by combining a composite language model (RCLM) for co-evolutionary feature extraction, an RNA Transformer-based pipeline with a denoising module for end-to-end structure generation, and a post-processing CSOR protocol for model selection and refinement.
Evaluation: Evaluated on global RNA tertiary structure prediction (TM-score, RMSD), secondary structure prediction (INF indices, Deformation Index), and physical geometry (MCQ, clash score). Benchmarked on an independent dataset of 41 RNA structures and the CASP16 experiment. Also evaluated for unsupervised contact map precision (Top-N precision) and computational efficiency (running time analysis).

Tool Usage: GRAPE-LM is a generative artificial intelligence framework that integrates a transformer-based conditional autoencoder with nucleic acid language models to perform single-round evolution and de novo generation of high-affinity RNA aptamers.
Architecture: Transformer-based conditional autoencoder (CAE) comprising a frozen pretrained nucleic acid language model (e.g., RNA-FM, RNA-BERT, Evo) encoder with a trainable adapter, an activity guidance module (multi-layer regression) to constrain the latent space using pseudo-activity scores, and an autoregressive transformer-based decoder.
Description: GRAPE-LM leverages CRISPR-Cas-based intracellular screening data (CRISmers) to guide the latent space of a nucleic acid language model, enabling the targeted generation of functional RNA aptamers without requiring multiple rounds of traditional SELEX.
Evaluation: Evaluated on three targets (human T cell receptor CD3, SARS-CoV-2 spike RBD, and human c-Myc). Metrics include recovery rate, sequence diversity, GC content deviation, Pearson's correlation coefficient for pseudo-activity prediction, retention rate, and binding affinity (Kd measured via flow cytometry and Microscale Thermophoresis). Benchmarked against RaptGen, AptaDiff, Apta-MCTS, AptaTrans, and traditional SELEX methods.

Tool Usage: A lightweight and efficient genomic foundation model that manages long-range dependencies in genomic sequences while minimizing computational costs. Supports sequences longer than 140,000 tokens on a single GPU with performance comparable to larger models.
Description: dnaGrinder is a lightweight Transformer encoder for DNA sequences. Encoder-only BERT-style architecture optimized for efficiency. (2024)
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction

Tool Usage: InstaDeep's multi-species genomics foundation model with 1Mb conNatural language at single-nucleotide resolution. Unifies representation learning, functional track prediction, genome annotation, and controllable sequence generation via masked diffusion language modeling.
Description: U-Net Transformer architecture with single-base tokenization for improved resolution. Based on BERT-style encoder. (InstaDeep, 2024)
Evaluation: 18 downstream genomic tasks; Chromatin accessibility; Promoter/enhancer prediction; Splicing benchmarks

Tool Usage: A 7B parameter encoder-only transformer from GenBio AI trained on 10.6B nucleotides from 796 species. Shows improvements across supervised, generative, and zero-shot tasks for functional genomics, synthetic biology, and drug development.
Description: AIDO.DNA uses 7B parameter Transformer encoder for DNA sequence modeling. Large-scale BERT-style encoder. (ModelScope, 2024)
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction; Zero-shot classification

Tool Usage: A 4B parameter generative genome foundation model trained on 600+ Gbp of metagenomic data from diverse ecosystems. Uses BPE tokenization for 150x faster generation than comparable models. Excels at representing microbial species and generating protein-coding genes constrained by evolutionary principles.
Description: A 4-billion-parameter generative genome foundation model trained on large-scale metagenomic assemblies to capture complex microbial diversity and genomic elements.
Evaluation: Microbial species discrimination, protein-coding functionality, and biosynthetic gene cluster (BGC) synthesis.

Tool Usage: A generative genomic foundation model with 1.2B parameters and 98k nucleotide conNatural language length, trained on 386B bp of eukaryotic DNA. Demonstrates strong performance on sequence recovery benchmarks and can generate protein-coding DNA sequences that translate into structurally plausible proteins.
Description: GENERator is a generative genomic foundation model with autoregressive Transformer decoder architecture. 1.2B parameters, generates DNA sequences autoregressively. (Cochrane et al., 2025)
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction

Tool Usage: Google DeepMind's unified genomic model that takes DNA sequences up to 1 million base pairs and predicts thousands of molecular properties including gene expression, splicing patterns, chromatin features, and contact maps. Achieves state-of-the-art performance on variant effect prediction tasks.
Description: Alphagenome from Google DeepMind uses Transformer encoder architecture as a DNA foundation model. Large-scale encoder for genomic understanding. (DeepMind, 2025)
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction; Splicing prediction; Contact map prediction; 1M base pair conNatural language

Tool Usage: A DNA language model that learns sequence conNatural language in the human genome. Demonstrates that gLMs initially learn token frequencies, providing insights into how genomic foundation models capture conNatural languageual relationships.
Description: GROVER uses Transformer encoder architecture with BPE tokenization for genomic sequences. BERT-style encoder with masked language modeling. (2024)
Evaluation: Token frequency learning; Genome conNatural language prediction; gLM benchmark evaluation

Tool Usage: 50M-2.5B parameter models trained on 3202 human genomes + 850 species
Description: The models are trained following BERT methodology. The main technique to train LM transformers with MLM is called bidirectional encoder representations from transformers (BERT). (Dalla-Torre et al., Nature Methods 2024)
Evaluation: 18 downstream genomic tasks; Chromatin accessibility; Promoter/enhancer prediction; Splicing benchmarks

Tool Usage: A bidirectional equivariant long-range DNA sequence model using Mamba (state space model) architecture. Designed to handle both DNA strands while maintaining reverse complement equivariance for improved genomic modeling.
Architecture: Bi-directional and reverse-complement (RC) equivariant blocks based on the Mamba selective SSM
Description: Caduceus uses bidirectional Mamba architecture, a state-space model (SSM) with RC-equivariance (reverse complement). Uses Mamba blocks instead of attention. (Schiff et al., ICML 2024)
Evaluation: Genomic sequence classification, epigenetic and regulatory annotation, and long-range variant effects on gene expression.

Tool Usage: A 40B parameter biological foundation model trained on 9.3 trillion DNA base pairs from all domains of life with 1 million token conNatural language window. Predicts functional impacts of genetic variation including noncoding pathogenic mutations and BRCA1 variants. Generates mitochondrial, prokaryotic, and eukaryotic sequences at genome scale.
Description: A biological foundation model trained on 9.3 trillion DNA base pairs spanning all domains of life for genomic prediction and design
Evaluation: BRCA1 variant prediction; Pathogenic mutation classification; Genome-scale generation; 1M conNatural language evaluationPathogenic variant effects, molecular/organismal fitness, and de novo sequence generation.

Tool Usage: BPE-based multi-species genomic model with 21x fewer parameters than DNABERT
Description: DNABERT-2 is an efficient multi-species genome foundation model based on BERT architecture with Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) tokenization and ALiBi positional encoding. Uses bidirectional encoder with masked language modeling. (Zhou et al., ICLR 2024)
Evaluation: Genome Understanding Evaluation (GUE) benchmark; Zero-shot species classification; TF binding site prediction

Tool Usage: 1M token conNatural language model at single-nucleotide resolution using Hyena architecture
Description: HyenaDNA uses Hyena operators (long convolution + gating mechanism), a state-space model architecture. Not attention-based Transformer. Handles context windows up to 1 million nucleotides. (Nguyen et al., NeurIPS 2023)
Evaluation: Species classification; Regulatory element prediction; 1M conNatural language benchmarks; Chromatin profile prediction

Tool Usage: Bidirectional transformer using k-mer tokenization for genomic sequence understanding
Description: We adapted the idea of Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) model to genomic DNA setting. We used the same model architecture as the BERT base, which consists of 12 Transformer layers with 768 hidden units and 12 attention heads. (Ji et al., Bioinformatics 2021)
Evaluation: Genome Understanding Evaluation (GUE) benchmark; Zero-shot species classification; TF binding site prediction

Tool Usage: iGWAS: Image-based genome-wide association of self-supervised deep phenotyping of retina fundus images
Architecture: Inception v3 backbone with ArcFace-like contrastive loss
Description: iGWAS uses hybrid Transformer/CNN architecture for imaging-GWAS analysis, combining vision and genomics. (2024)
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction

Tool Usage: ContIG, ContIG: Self-supervised Multimodal Contrastive Learning for Medical Imaging with Genetics
Description: ContIG uses contrastive learning with dual encoders for image-genomics alignment. Separate CNN/ViT image encoder and genomics encoder. (Taleb et al., 2021)
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction

Tool Usage: PM1: A Foundation Model Fusing Genotype, Phenotype, and Image for Precision Medicine
Description: Transformer encoder for multimodal prediction.
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction

Tool Usage: A foundation model for learning genetic associations from brain imaging phenotypes
Description: Transformer encoder for multimodal medical image analysis.
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction

Tool Usage: Genetic InfoMax: Exploring Mutual Information Maximization in High-Dimensional Imaging Genetics Studies
Architecture: 3D CNN MRI encoder + Swin-transformer genetic encoder + Reconstruction head
Description: GIM uses mutual information maximization with dual encoders for image-genomics learning. Contrastive approach with separate modality encoders. (2023)
Evaluation: Loci discovery (GWAS), disease risk prediction (e.g., stroke, cancer), and sex classification.

Tool Usage: AlzCLIP, Integration of Genetic and Imaging Data for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis and Interpretation
Description: AlzCLIP uses CLIP-style dual encoder for Alzheimer's disease brain imaging with vision-language alignment. Contrastive learning on neuroimaging-text pairs. (2025)
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction

Tool Usage: RadGenNets: Deep learning-based radiogenomics model for gene mutation prediction in lung cancer
Architecture: Fusion of CNNs (EfficientNet, SENet, ResNeXt) and Dense Neural Networks
Description: A multimodal radiogenomics framework that integrates PET/CT imaging with clinical and genomic data to predict gene mutations in non-small cell lung cancer patients.
Evaluation: Lung cancer gene mutation prediction (specifically EGFR, KRAS, and ALK mutations).

Tool Usage: An upgrade to the famous Enformer model. Borzoi takes a DNA sequence and predicts gene expression tracks across thousands of cell types.
Architecture: Convolutional tower + self-attention blocks + U-net upsampling
Description: A sequence-based model that predicts cell-type-specific RNA-seq coverage from DNA to interpret variant effects across multiple regulatory layers
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction (eQTLs, sQTLs, paQTLs) and distal enhancer-gene interaction mapping

Tool Usage: GPT architecture on 33M+ cells for cell annotation integration and perturbation prediction
Description: scGPT is based on a generative pretrained transformer (GPT) architecture. The core component of scGPT contains stacked transformer blocks with specialized attention masks for generative training. Uses transformer decoders by making sequential predictions with masked attention. (Cui et al., Nature Methods 2024)
Evaluation: Cell type annotation; Gene perturbation; Batch integration; Multi-omics integration; 33M cell benchmark

Tool Usage: This is a "Generalist" medical foundation model trained on 3.3 million images across 7 modalities, including both Pathology patches (H&E) and TBP.
Architecture: Dual-network teacher-student ViT framework with a dynamic memory module
Description: A multimodal, multi-specialty foundation model trained using self-supervised learning (SSL) to unify the interpretation of diverse imaging types through a shared latent space.
Evaluation: Detection of colorectal adenocarcinoma, breast cancer, and various tissue types on histopathology patches.

Tool Usage: Dermatopathology foundation model pretrained on histopathology tiles for microscopic skin tissue analysis and cancer classification.
Architecture: ViT-Large Image Encoder + Masked Latent Regressor
Description: PanDerm extends unified dermatology representations to histopathology images, supporting skin cancer diagnosis and pathology-level phenotype analysis across multiple datasets.
Evaluation: Skin cancer pathology classification

Tool Usage: ViT-Large trained on 460M tiles from 55K+ public slides for biomarker prediction (vision-only)
Description: Phikon-v2 uses Vision Transformer encoder trained with iBOT self-supervised learning on 460M pathology image tiles. Encoder-only ViT design. (Filiot et al., 2024)
Evaluation: Tile-level classification; Linear probe evaluation; TCGA benchmarks

Tool Usage: Google's self-supervised framework combining BiT + SimCLR for medical imaging (vision-only)
Description: REMEDIS uses Vision Transformer encoder with self-supervised contrastive learning (SimCLR) for medical imaging. Encoder-only ResNet/ViT architecture. (Azizi et al., Nature Biomedical Engineering 2023)
Evaluation: Histopathology classification

Tool Usage: Foundation model on 1.5M WSIs from MSK enabling pan-cancer detection with 0.95 AUC (vision-only)
Description: Virchow employs the Vision Transformer 'huge' architecture (ViT-H/14) with 632 million parameters. Trained using DINOv2 self-supervised learning on 1.5M WSIs. Encoder-only design with self-attention. (Vorontsov et al., Nature Medicine 2024)
Evaluation: Pan-cancer classification; Biomarker prediction; Tile-level benchmarks; 17 tissue types evaluation

Tool Usage: Weakly supervised foundation model on 60K WSIs across 19 anatomical sites (vision-only)
Description: CHIEF (Clinical Histopathology Imaging Evaluation Foundation) uses Vision Transformer encoder with self-supervised learning for computational pathology. Encoder-only ViT design. (Wang et al., Nature 2024)
Evaluation: Histopathology classification

Tool Usage: Hybrid CNN-Swin Transformer trained on 15M TCGA/PAIP patches (vision-only)
Description: CTransPath uses Swin Transformer encoder architecture with hierarchical feature maps. Self-supervised pretraining with semantically-relevant contrastive learning. Encoder-only design. (Wang et al., 2022)
Evaluation: TCGA classification; Multi-organ evaluation; Transfer learning benchmarks

Tool Usage: Hierarchical Vision Transformer leveraging WSI structure on 104M images (vision-only)
Description: HIPT (Hierarchical Image Pyramid Transformer) uses hierarchical Vision Transformer encoder architecture at multiple scales (256‚Üí4096‚ÜíWSI). Encoder-only design with multi-scale self-attention. (Chen et al., CVPR 2022)
Evaluation: Hierarchical WSI analysis; Survival prediction; Subtyping; TCGA benchmarks

Tool Usage: Whole-slide foundation model trained on 1.3B image tiles from 171K WSIs (vision-only)
Description: Prov-GigaPath uses Vision Transformer encoder with LongNet-style dilated attention for processing gigapixel pathology images. Encoder-only design trained on 1.3B image tiles from 170k WSIs. (Xu et al., Nature 2024)
Evaluation: 17 genomic prediction tasks; 9 cancer subtyping tasks; Slide-level classification; LongNet evaluation

Tool Usage: Self-supervised vision model on 100M+ images from 100K diagnostic whole-slide images (vision-only)
Description: UNI is a general-purpose self-supervised vision encoder based on Vision Transformer (ViT-Large) architecture. Pretrained using DINOv2 self-supervised learning on 100M+ images from 100K+ WSIs. Encoder-only ViT with 307M parameters. (Chen et al., Nature Medicine 2024)
Evaluation: 34 clinical tasks in anatomic pathology; ROI classification; Slide-level classification; Segmentation (SegPath); Retrieval

Tool Usage: Multimodal (pathology + omics) pretraining with masking/reconstruction objectives (paper positions MORPHEUS as a multimodal pathology/omics model).
Description: MORPHEUS uses hybrid CNN/Transformer architecture for morphological analysis in medical imaging. (2024)
Evaluation: phenotype association

Tool Usage: It takes routine H&E whole-slide images as input and uses a large pathology foundation model to predict hundreds to thousands of genomic and molecular biomarkers (e.g., gene mutations) in a pan-cancer, high-throughput screening setting.
Architecture: Vision foundation encoder (Virchow2) + attention-based slide-level aggregation network
Description: Transformer encoder for medical screening tasks.
Evaluation: Biomarker screening; genomic alteration classification; pathway phenotype detection

Tool Usage: ViT-based ROI encoder + attention-based slide encoder with multimodal contrastive alignment to molecular profiles
Description: Threads uses Transformer encoder for molecular-driven whole slide representation learning in spatial transcriptomics. (2024)
Evaluation: Variant effect prediction

Tool Usage: Virtual multiplex immunofluorescence (mIF) prediction from routine H&E whole-slide images to enable cell-level immune and stromal profiling without physical staining
Architecture: ViT foundation encoder (H-optimus-0 / UNIv2 / CTransPath) embedded in a U-Net style encoder decoder (ViTMatte / UNETR variants) with multi-head output for marker regression
Description: MIPHEI-ViT predicts spatially resolved mIF protein expression maps from H&E images using a ViT-based encoder decoder architecture, enabling single-cell marker inference and large-scale immune profiling.
Evaluation: Virtual staining (image translation); protein expression prediction; cell-level classification; cross-domain generalization

Tool Usage: ROSIE takes H&E images as input and computationally generates in silico mIF images, predicting the spatial expression of dozens of protein markers to enable immune cell phenotyping and tumor microenvironment analysis without performing physical mIF experiments. The model extracts morphological embeddings from H&E patches (CNN-based vision encoder). These embeddings are mapped through a generative / regression head to output multi-channel mIF marker maps (one channel per protein marker).
Description: Transformer encoder for medical imaging.
Evaluation: Histopathology classification; Spatial gene expression prediction

Tool Usage: H&E to protein expression model built on a pathology foundation model. Generates virtual spatial proteomics from histopathology, predicting expression of 40 biomarkers spanning immune, structural, and functional programs with high accuracy across 34 tissue types.
Architecture: Vision encoder (pathology foundation model) + tile-level regression head for protein prediction; downstream multimodal risk model using attention-based MIL over predicted protein maps
Description: HEX predicts high-plex spatial protein expression maps ( virtual CODEX ) directly from H&E images and leverages the inferred spatial proteomics to stratify patient risk and immunotherapy response.
Evaluation: Protein expression prediction; prognosis prediction; immunotherapy response prediction

Tool Usage: Virtual multiplex immunofluorescence (mIF) translation from routine H&E whole-slide images to enable population-scale tumor immune microenvironment (TIME) profiling and downstream biomarker association studies
Architecture: Patch-based encoder decoder image-to-image translation network built on Nested U-Net; outputs 21 protein channels per patch and stitches to slide-level maps
Description: Transformer encoder for time-series medical data.
Evaluation: Image translation (virtual staining); protein activation segmentation; biomarker association; patient stratification

Tool Usage: It uses LLM-derived domain knowledge to refine gene features and combine them with visual embeddings from pathology images. It aims to improve gene expression prediction from H&E by integrating gene-level Natural languageual information (e.g., descriptions/embeddings) with image features.
Architecture: Image encoders + fusion module for domain-robust ST modeling
Description: DomainST uses Transformer encoder for domain adaptation in spatial transcriptomics across different technologies and tissues. (2024)
Evaluation: ST prediction / generalization evaluation across domains (per paper).

Tool Usage: The tool leverages pretrained pathology foundation model embeddings and spatial conNatural language to predict virtual single-cell level gene expression from H&E histopathology images using spot-level supervision from spatial transcriptomics data, effectively enabling inferred single-cell transcriptomic profiles from standard histology
Description: DeepSpot2Cell uses CNN for spot detection in spatial transcriptomics. Convolutional architecture. (2024)
Evaluation: Histopathology classification; Spatial gene expression prediction

Tool Usage: CLIP model linking H&E with spatial transcriptomics (spatial transcriptomics not mIF)
Description: OmiCLIP uses CLIP-style dual encoder for multiomics-image alignment. Separate encoders for pathology images and multi-omics data with contrastive learning. (2024)
Evaluation: Zero-shot classification; Image-Natural language retrieval

Tool Usage: Self-supervised foundation model for 175 protein markers across 8 mIF platforms (vision-only no Natural language)
Description: KRONOS uses Transformer encoder for learning patch feature from spatial proteomics data. (2024)
Evaluation: Multi-task benchmark evaluation

Tool Usage: Unpaired image-to-image translation to convert multi-channel fluorescence microscopy images into virtual H&E stained histopathology images
Description: This CycleGAN-based model translates fluorescence microscopy images into H&E-like images without paired training data, preserving tissue morphology while adopting H&E color and texture characteristics.
Evaluation: Virtual staining; qualitative morphology preservation; visual realism assessment

Tool Usage: A multi-scale generative and predictive model for spatial transcriptomics (which uses images like mIF).
Architecture: ViT image encoder + scPRINT gene backbone + Hierarchical Transformers + Diffusion decoder
Description: A multi-scale model that integrates cellular morphology, gene expression, and spatial context for predictive and generative tasks in spatial transcriptomics
Evaluation: Cell classification, spatial clustering, gene expression prediction, biomarker status prediction (breast cancer), and cell image synthesis

Tool Usage: A heirarchical graph transformer foundation model for spatial transcriptomics and proteomics.
Architecture: Hierarchical Graph Transformer (intra-cellular and inter-cellular levels)
Description: A hierarchical graph transformer foundation model designed for spatial transcriptomics and proteomics that captures both intra-cellular gene/protein co-expression and inter-cellular spatial interactions.
Evaluation: Tissue segmentation, cell-type annotation, and gene/protein expression imputation across mouse brain and human cancer datasets.

Tool Usage: Discusses a general-purpose MRI foundation model (also known as UMBIF) used for grading brain tumors across multiple MRI sequences.
Description: MRI-CORE uses Transformer encoder as a foundation model for MRI analysis. Self-supervised pretraining on diverse MRI datasets. (2024)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: A "Pan-Tumor" oncology model. It overcomes data scarcity by using synthetic data to detect and stage tumors across ten different organ systems.
Description: Leverages 30,000 synthetic CT scans (PASTA-Gen) with pixel-level lesion masks and structured reports.
Evaluation: 45 oncology tasks; Tumor detection; Survival prediction; Report generation.

Tool Usage: A universal, text-guided assistant. It performs segmentation via text prompts (e.g., "segment tumor") rather than requiring spatial cues like clicks.
Description: Full-parameter fine-tuning on 33 datasets across 10 modalities to handle medical concepts without spatial prompts.
Evaluation: Zero-shot Dice score; Semantic-driven segmentation; External dataset generalization.

Tool Usage: Uses "geometric context" priors to learn representations. It is used to reduce labeled data dependency for complex tasks like volume registration.
Description: Implicitly encodes geometric relations between organs using volume contrastive learning on 160,000 CT volumes.
Evaluation: 51 tasks: Segmentation, Classification, Registration, Vision-Language.

Tool Usage: While focused on MRI, this "Founder" model provides the brain-side data needed to create the OCT-to-MRI link.
Description: BrainSegFounder uses ViT/CNN architecture for brain MRI segmentation foundation model. Primarily encoder-based design. (2024)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: Classification, segmentation, and object detection
Architecture: ResNet-50 or ViT-B/16 backbones with a second-order graph matching SSL formulation
Description: A general-purpose medical vision foundation model pre-trained on 1.3 million multi-modal images using graph-based structural constraints.
Evaluation: Brain tumor classification/segmentation, prostate segmentation, and heart structure segmentation.

Tool Usage: A vision-transformer-based foundation model used for "multi-modal" synthesis (creating PET from MRI or vice versa).
Description: ResVit is a hybrid architecture combining ResNet convolutional layers with Vision Transformer attention. (2022)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: 70B model trained on 129B tokens from biomedical and clinical sources
Description: Me-LLaMA uses LLaMA decoder-only architecture with continued pretraining on large-scale medical corpora and instruction tuning. (Xie et al., 2024)
Evaluation: Perplexity evaluation; Generation quality assessment

Tool Usage: Microsoft's modular clinical SLM collection (3.8B params); 5 domain experts merged with SLERP; MIT licensed for deployment
Description: MediPhi-Instruct is based on Microsoft Phi decoder-only architecture with medical instruction tuning. Small but capable medical LLM. (2024)
Evaluation: Perplexity evaluation; Generation quality assessment

Tool Usage: Hippocrates is an open-source framework for developing medical large language models, introducing the Hippo family of 7B models tailored for medical reasoning and question answering.
Architecture: Mistral 7B and LLaMA2 7B backbones adapted to the medical domain via continual pre-training, instruction tuning, and Reinforcement Learning from AI Feedback (RLAIF).
Description: The Hippocrates framework democratizes medical AI by providing unrestricted access to its training datasets, codebase, model checkpoints, and evaluation protocols. The associated Hippo models leverage preference learning mapped to clinical standards to enhance medical domain proficiency.
Evaluation: Evaluated on six major medical question-answering benchmarks including MedQA, MedMCQA, PubMedQA, and USMLE Steps 1-3 using the EleutherAI LM Evaluation Harness. Performance was assessed via zero-shot and 5-shot accuracy, alongside uncertainty quantification to measure model confidence.

Tool Usage: Answering medical questions, generating clinical notes, and providing clinical decision support.
Description: Clinical Camel is based on LLaMA decoder-only architecture, fine-tuned for clinical dialogue using instruction tuning. Autoregressive Transformer decoder. (Toma et al., 2023)
Evaluation: Perplexity evaluation; Generation quality assessment

Tool Usage: Med42 is a medical large language model fine-tuned to enhance medical knowledge retrieval, clinical reasoning, and question-answering capabilities. It evaluates and compares the effectiveness of full-parameter fine-tuning versus parameter-efficient tuning (LoRA) for adapting general LLMs to the medical domain.
Architecture: Built upon the Llama-2 backbone (7B and 70B parameter versions). The pretraining strategy relies on Llama-2's robust general-domain pretraining. The modal-specific design involves instruction fine-tuning on a curated compilation of open medical datasets, employing both full-parameter updates and Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) applied to linear layers for comparative analysis.
Description: The paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of fine-tuning methodologies for medical LLMs. The resulting flagship model, Med42 (70B), achieves a zero-shot accuracy of 72% on USMLE datasets, establishing that full-parameter fine-tuning generally outperforms parameter-efficient methods in complex medical reasoning contexts.
Evaluation: Evaluated using zero-shot accuracy on multiple medical benchmark datasets, including MedQA, HeadQA, MedMCQA, PubMedQA, MMLU clinical topics, and the US Medical Licensing Examination (USMLE). Also includes an evaluation of computational efficiency and scaling by comparing 7B and 70B parameter models under full-parameter versus LoRA fine-tuning constraints.

Tool Usage: Suite of clinical LLMs (8B/70B) built on Llama3 achieving 94.5% on medical benchmarks; tops Clinical Elo Rating Leaderboard
Description: Med42-v2 is a decoder-only LLM based on LLaMA architecture for medical question answering. Fine-tuned with medical instructions. (M42 Health, 2024)
Evaluation: Perplexity evaluation; Generation quality assessment

Tool Usage: Foresight is a general-purpose model for biomedical concept modelling that can be used for real-world risk forecasting, virtual trials, and clinical research to study the progression of disorders, to simulate interventions and counterfactuals, and for educational purposes.
Description: Foresight uses Transformer encoder for predictive EHR modeling and clinical forecasting. (2024)
Evaluation: Task-specific fine-tuning

Tool Usage: BioMedLM is a 2.7 billion parameter language model that produces strong multiple-choice biomedical question-answering results and provides useful answers to patient questions on medical topics.
Architecture: GPT-style (GPT-2 based) autoregressive model with 2.7 billion parameters. Pretrained exclusively on PubMed abstracts and full articles using a domain-specific biomedical Byte-Pair Encoding (BPE) tokenizer.
Description: BioMedLM is a domain-specific large language model trained exclusively on biomedical text to serve as a transparent, privacy-preserving, and computationally economical foundation for biomedical NLP applications.
Evaluation: Tasks: Biomedical question-answering. Benchmarks: MedMCQA, MMLU Medical Genetics exam, BioASQ, PubMedQA, MedQA.

Tool Usage: Google's Natural language-only medical LLM for clinical reasoning and EHR understanding; achieves SOTA on MedQA
Description: MedGemma-27B is based on Gemma 27B decoder-only architecture with medical fine-tuning. Large-scale autoregressive Transformer. (Google, 2024)
Evaluation: Perplexity evaluation; Generation quality assessment

Tool Usage: EPFL's 70B parameter model adapted from Llama-2 on 48.1B medical tokens from guidelines and PubMed
Description: Meditron-70B is based on LLaMA-2 70B, a decoder-only Transformer architecture. Fine-tuned on curated medical corpora using causal language modeling. (Chen et al., 2023)
Evaluation: USMLE; MedMCQA; PubMedQA; Medical reasoning benchmarks

Tool Usage: Open-source LLM fine-tuned on 4.8 million biomedical papers for medical domain reasoning
Description: PMC-LLaMA is LLaMA fine-tuned on 4.8M PubMed Central biomedical papers. Uses LLaMA decoder-only Transformer architecture with continued pretraining. (Wu et al., 2023)
Evaluation: Medical QA; PubMedQA; MedQA; BioASQ; Medical instruction following

Tool Usage: Mistral-based model with first multilingual medical evaluation across 7 languages
Description: BioMistral is based on Mistral-7B decoder-only architecture with sliding window attention. Fine-tuned on PubMed Central for biomedical tasks. (Labrak et al., 2024)
Evaluation: PubMedQA; MedQA; Medical NLI; Clinical summarization

Tool Usage: Open-source medical conversational AI fine-tuned on 160K medical QA training entries
Description: MedAlpaca is based on LLaMA decoder-only architecture fine-tuned with medical instruction data using self-instruct methodology. (Han et al., 2023)
Evaluation: Perplexity evaluation; Generation quality assessment

Tool Usage: NYUTron s performance on three clinical tasks (in-patient mortality prediction, comorbidity index prediction and readmission prediction) and two operational tasks (insurance claim denial prediction and inpatient LOS prediction)
Description: NYUTron uses BERT-style encoder architecture trained on clinical notes. Bidirectional Transformer encoder achieving clinical deployment for readmission prediction. (Jiang et al., Nature 2023)
Evaluation: Downstream task fine-tuning

Tool Usage: Med-PaLM 2 is a large language model designed to retrieve medical knowledge, reason over it, and answer medical questions comparably to physicians.
Architecture: Dense decoder-only Transformer based on PaLM 2. Pretrained on diverse text corpora and finetuned on medical domains using advanced prompting strategies such as ensemble refinement.
Description: Med-PaLM 2 leverages the foundational PaLM 2 model with medical-domain finetuning to achieve state-of-the-art performance in medical question answering, significantly surpassing its predecessor Med-PaLM and reaching expert-level reasoning capabilities.
Evaluation: Evaluated on MedQA (USMLE-style questions), MedMCQA, PubMedQA, and MMLU clinical topics. It also underwent detailed human evaluations on 1,066 consumer medical questions and 240 long-form adversarial questions to assess clinical utility against physician answers.

Tool Usage: Extracting information from clinical notes, predicting clinical outcomes, and identifying patient cohorts.
Description: GatorTron is a large clinical language model using BERT-style encoder architecture with 8.9B parameters. Trained with masked language modeling on clinical text from UF Health. Encoder-only Transformer. (Yang et al., npj Digital Medicine 2022)
Evaluation: Clinical NER; Clinical inference; Medical QA; STS; 5 clinical NLP tasks

Tool Usage: This work develops and evaluates representations of clinical notes using bidirectional transformers
Description: ClinicalBERT is a BERT model pre-trained on clinical notes from MIMIC-III. Uses same encoder-only Transformer architecture as BERT with masked language modeling on clinical text. (Alsentzer et al., Clinical NLP Workshop 2019)
Evaluation: Clinical NER; Readmission prediction; ICD coding; MIMIC-III benchmarks

Tool Usage: Generative transformer for biomedical Natural language mining achieving 78% accuracy on PubMedQA
Description: BioGPT is a domain-specific generative pre-trained Transformer language model for biomedical text generation using GPT-2 decoder architecture. Autoregressive, unidirectional, decoder-only. (Luo et al., Briefings in Bioinformatics 2022)
Evaluation: PubMed QA; Relation extraction; Document classification; End-to-end generation

Tool Usage: PubMedBERT is a domain-specific language model designed for biomedical natural language processing. It challenges the prevailing mixed-domain paradigm by demonstrating that pretraining large language models from scratch entirely on biomedical text yields superior performance on downstream tasks.
Architecture: Backbone is BERT-Base (12 transformer layers, 110M parameters). Pretraining strategy involves Masked Language Modeling with 15% whole-word masking (WWM) and Next Sentence Prediction, trained from scratch on 14 million PubMed abstracts. Modality-specific design includes a custom domain-specific vocabulary built with the WordPiece algorithm generated strictly from the biomedical training corpus rather than reusing a general-domain vocabulary.
Description: The paper introduces PubMedBERT and the Biomedical Language Understanding & Reasoning Benchmark (BLURB), a comprehensive benchmark for PubMed-based NLP applications. It establishes that for domains with abundant unlabeled text, pretraining from scratch provides a stronger foundation than continual pretraining of general-domain language models.
Evaluation: Evaluated on the BLURB benchmark, which includes 13 publicly available datasets across 6 biomedical NLP tasks: Named Entity Recognition (NER), Evidence-Based Medical Information Extraction (PICO), Relation Extraction (RE), Sentence Similarity, Document Classification, and Question Answering (QA). Also includes ablation studies on pretraining strategies (e.g., using whole-word masking vs. subword masking) and fine-tuning methods.

Tool Usage: MultiMedQA is a large language model adapted to the medical domain to answer professional, research, and consumer medical questions.
Architecture: 540-billion parameter Pathways Language Model (PaLM) backbone, initially instruction-tuned (Flan-PaLM), and further aligned to the medical domain using a parameter-efficient approach called instruction prompt tuning.
Description: The study introduces MultiMedQA, a comprehensive benchmark for medical question answering, and evaluates the model using both automated metrics and a detailed human evaluation framework.
Evaluation: Evaluated on the MultiMedQA benchmark (which includes MedQA/USMLE, MedMCQA, PubMedQA, MMLU clinical topics, and HealthSearchQA). Metrics include multiple-choice accuracy and human evaluation scoring along axes of factuality, comprehension, reasoning, possible harm, and bias.

Tool Usage: Domain-specific BERT pretrained on PubMed abstracts and PMC full-Natural language articles
Description: BioBERT has the same structure as BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers). Initialized with BERT weights and pre-trained on biomedical corpora (PubMed abstracts + PMC full-text). Uses 12 Transformer encoder layers with masked language modeling. (Lee et al., Bioinformatics 2020)
Evaluation: NER; Relation extraction; QA (BioASQ); PubMed benchmarks; BLURB evaluation

Tool Usage: This study introduces EyeCLIP, a multimodal vision language foundation model pretrained on multi-modal ophthalmic images and partial clinical Natural language using self-supervised reconstruction and contrastive learning, enabling generalizable, few-shot/zero-shot disease detection and cross-modal reasoning.
Description: EyeCLIP is a multimodal vision-language foundation model using CLIP-style dual encoder architecture. Self-supervised reconstruction and contrastive learning for ophthalmic images and clinical text. (npj Digital Medicine 2025)
Evaluation: Zero-shot classification; Image-Natural language retrieval

Tool Usage: Cardiac CT multimodal model combining 3D MAE with contrastive vision-language learning
Description: Cardiac-CLIP uses CLIP-style dual encoder for cardiac imaging with ViT image encoder and text encoder. Contrastive pretraining on cardiac reports. (2024)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: A scalable, open transformer model for 3D CT. It extract robust volumetric features using dual-attention to manage extreme token scaling.
Description: Combines DINO-style distillation with SigLIP vision-language alignment and anisotropic patch embeddings.
Evaluation: Transferable representations; Zero-shot/Fine-tuned benchmarks.

Tool Usage: First CT-focused contrastive vision-language model with CT-RATE dataset of 25692 chest CTs
Description: CT-CLIP uses CLIP-style dual encoder with 3D Vision Transformer for CT volumes and text encoder. Contrastive learning for CT-report alignment. (Hamamci et al., 2024)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: 3D abdominal CT vision-language model using EHR and reports for 692 phenotype predictions
Description: Merlin, a novel 3D Vision-Language Model (VLM) designed to automate the interpretation of abdominal CT scans. It addresses the growing volume of complex imaging studies and the current shortage of radiologists.
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: Joint segmentation detection and recognition across 9 biomedical modalities using Natural language prompts (excludes mIF)
Description: BiomedParse uses ViT encoder with decoder for biomedical image segmentation across 9 modalities. Encoder-decoder architecture for joint detection, segmentation, and recognition. (Microsoft Research, 2024)
Evaluation: Perplexity evaluation; Generation quality assessment

Tool Usage: A radiology foundation model that integrates Natural languageual inputs with 2D and 3D medical imaging to perform diverse radiologic tasks such as diagnosis, visual question answering, report generation, and reasoning‚Äö√Ñ√Æwithin a unified multimodal AI framework.
Description: RadFM is a radiology foundation model integrating 2D and 3D medical imaging with text. Uses MedCLIP/3D encoders feeding into LLaMA decoder for report generation and VQA. (Wu et al., Nature Communications 2025)
Evaluation: 2D/3D radiology benchmarks; Report generation; VQA; Multi-modal understanding

Tool Usage: Visual Question Answering (VQA) and reasoning
Architecture: Transformer decoder with MoE and custom modality encoders
Description: A family of multimodal models specialized for medicine through self-training, web search integration, and tailored encoders
Evaluation: Melanoma diagnosis, lesion change detection

Tool Usage: Google's generalist biomedical multimodal model trained on MultiMedBench benchmark. Handles diverse data types including clinical language, medical imaging (radiology, pathology, dermatology, mammography), and genomics via natural language instructions.
Architecture: Pretrained PaLM LLM and ViT encoders aligned through multimodal finetuning
Description: Medical visual question answering on CT images covering head, abdomen, and chest regions.
Evaluation: Diagnosis, few-shot transfer.

Tool Usage: explicitly a biomedical vision-language foundation model; training data covers microscopy and histology, and the provided label set includes hematoxylin and eosin histopathology
Description: BiomedCLIP uses CLIP-style architecture with PubMedBERT text encoder and ViT-B/16 image encoder. Dual encoder design with contrastive learning on PMC-15M image-text pairs from PubMed Central. (Zhang et al., 2023)
Evaluation: PMC-15M benchmark; Zero-shot classification; Image-Natural language retrieval; Medical VQA

Tool Usage: Uses a dedicated ECG encoder to transform raw 12-lead ECG signals into token-level representations aligned with a frozen biomedical LLM; supports text ECG joint pretraining, ECG-QA, and zero-shot ECG interpretation tasks.
Description: ECG-FM is a transformer-based ECG foundation model that learns physiologically meaningful representations via hybrid self-supervision, capturing both low-level waveform morphology and higher-order cardiac semantics. The pretrained encoder supports efficient finetuning and linear probing across diverse ECG interpretation and cardiac function tasks, with strong cross-dataset generalization.
Evaluation: ECG diagnostic classification (diagnostic / rhythm / form), ECG question answering, zero-shot disease detection

Tool Usage: First multimodal ECG instruction tuning framework for report generation; benchmarked with 9 LLMs on 800K+ ECG reports
Architecture: ECG Encoder + LLM Backbone with Modality Alignment
Description: MEIT (Multimodal ECG Instruction Tuning) injects ECG embeddings into the self-attention layers of decoder-only LLMs via a lightweight concatenation-based alignment mechanism, enabling instruction-following, autoregressive ECG report generation that aligns with clinical language and expert annotations.
Evaluation: ECG report generation quality, zero-shot cross-dataset transfer, robustness to signal perturbation, alignment with human expert evaluation

Tool Usage: Multimodal LLM using contrastive learning to align ECG waveforms with cardiology reports; zero-shot retrieval
Architecture: ECG ViT Encoder + MLP Adapter + Vicuna-13B
Description: ECG-Chat is a multimodal large language model for cardiology that integrates a contrastively pretrained ECG encoder with a decoder-only LLM. ECG features are embedded as tokens and injected into the LLM via a projection layer, enabling long-form ECG diagnostic report generation, ECG question answering, and conversational interaction. The system further incorporates diagnosis-driven prompting and GraphRAG to reduce hallucinations and improve clinical fidelity.
Evaluation: ECG interpretation (disease, rhythm, form classification); ECG text retrieval; long-form ECG report generation; multi-turn ECG dialogue

Tool Usage: Multimodal contrastive pretraining on 660K ECG-Natural language pairs using RAG-based LLM pipeline for Natural language generation
Description: ESI is an ECG foundation model pretrained using ECG text pairs generated by a retrieval-augmented LLM pipeline (CQA). It jointly optimizes contrastive alignment and captioning losses to integrate waveform patterns, demographic context, and clinical semantics, producing robust ECG embeddings for zero-shot inference, linear probing, and fine-tuning.
Evaluation: Arrhythmia diagnosis, zero-shot ECG classification, ECG-based subject identification

Tool Usage: Retrieves/predicts gene-related signals by combining gene text descriptions with learned gene representations; uses an attention-based gene encoder and cross-modal matching to query gene signals.
Description: GeneQuery is presented as a framework to query gene representations using text and learned embeddings (not a generative decoder).
Evaluation: Evaluated on gene-level query/retrieval-style tasks as described in paper.

Tool Usage: Tool-augmented LLM using NCBI Web APIs for genomics question answering via in-conNatural language learning
Description: GeneGPT uses GPT-style decoder architecture augmented with NCBI API tools for gene-related QA tasks. Based on autoregressive Transformer decoder. (Jin et al., 2023)

Tool Usage: BioT5 is a comprehensive pre-training framework that integrates molecules, proteins, and natural language to enhance drug discovery and biological understanding.
Architecture: T5-v1.1-base architecture (252M parameters) utilizing a shared encoder and decoder. It employs separate vocabularies for molecule SELFIES, protein FASTA, and natural language text. Pre-trained using a multi-task approach including masked span recovery and bidirectional translation between bio-sequences and structured text descriptions.
Description: BioT5 leverages SELFIES for robust molecular representations and extracts knowledge from unstructured biological literature and structured databases. It distinguishes between structured and unstructured knowledge to effectively capture underlying relations and properties of bio-entities.
Evaluation: Evaluated on 15 downstream tasks across three categories: single-instance prediction (molecule property prediction on MoleculeNet, protein property prediction on PEER), multi-instance prediction (drug-target interaction on BioSNAP, Human, BindingDB; protein-protein interaction on Yeast, Human), and cross-modal generation (molecule captioning and text-based molecule generation on ChEBI-20). Metrics include AUROC, AUPRC, Accuracy, BLEU, ROUGE, METEOR, Text2Mol, Exact Match, Levenshtein, FCD, and Validity.

Tool Usage: Multi-agent LLM framework designed specifically for automated cell type annotation.
Description: Transformer encoder for medical image analysis.

Tool Usage: CellWhisperer is an artificial intelligence model and software tool that enables interactive, chat-based exploration and interrogation of single-cell RNA-sequencing data using natural language
Architecture: Multimodal contrastive learning architecture (adapted from CLIP and LiT) using Geneformer for transcriptome embedding and BioBERT for text embedding. A LLaVA-style two-layer adapter module projects the joint embeddings into the token space of a Mistral 7B large language model for chat generation
Description: CellWhisperer establishes a multimodal embedding of transcriptomes and textual annotations via contrastive learning on 1 million RNA-seq profiles. This embedding informs a fine-tuned large language model that answers user-provided questions about cells and genes in natural-language chats, integrated with the CELLxGENE browser
Evaluation: Zero-shot prediction of cell types, diseases, tissues, and organs (AUROC, Accuracy, F1 score); dataset integration and batch effect correction (average silhouette width, average bio score); gene set enrichment correlation (Pearson correlation, Kolmogorov-Smirnoff statistic); perplexity-based evaluation of chat responses on matched vs. mismatched transcriptomes.

Tool Usage: Method converting gene expression profiles to Natural language sentences for LLM fine-tuning (up to 27B params)

Evaluation: Task-specific fine-tuning

Tool Usage: Attention-based foundation model on 30-104M single cells for network biology and drug discovery
Description: Architecture Type: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT). The rank value encoding of each single cell's transcriptome proceeds through N layers of transformer encoder units. Pretraining was accomplished using a masked learning objective where 15% of the genes were masked. (Theodoris et al., Nature 2023)
Evaluation: Cell type classification; Gene network inference; Disease state prediction; 104M cells (v2)

Evaluation: Spatial domain identification; cell type annotation; spatial pattern interpretation
