Description: 6-class skin lesion image classification (PAD-UFES-20) incorporating patient clinical history.
Evaluation: Segmentation, disease classification.

Tool Usage: This study proposes RetFiner, a self-supervised vision language refinement framework that leverages Natural languageual supervision to enhance OCT foundation models, enabling population-specific adaptation and improved downstream performance without supervised fine-tuning.
Description: RetFiner uses dual encoder (ViT + BERT) for vision-language refinement in ophthalmology. Contrastive alignment with refinement. (2024)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: A brand new 2025 foundation model specifically designed to be "truly multimodal," connecting different views of the eye to systemic health.
Description: Transformer encoder for medical image representation.
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: It shows how eye photos can predict systemic health (heart/kidney) usually seen on CTs. Once the AI understands that "Body Health = Eye Health," doctors use a specialized model like RETFound to do the high-precision diagnosis on the eye photo itself. However, a pitfall is that CT isn't directly mentioned in the paper. However, the models have been trained on low feature distinction objects with RETFound being also trained on retinal images.
Description: RETFound uses Vision Transformer (ViT-Large) architecture pretrained with Masked Autoencoder (MAE) self-supervised learning on 1.6 million retinal images. Encoder-only design. (Zhou et al., Nature 2023)
Evaluation: Disease detection AUROC; Diabetic retinopathy; Glaucoma; AMD; Systemic disease prediction; Label efficiency

Tool Usage: Virtual staining / image-to-image translation to convert in vivo OCT images of human skin into H&E-like histopathology images
Architecture: CycleGAN-based conditional GAN: two ResNet encoder decoder generators + two PatchGAN discriminators, with auxiliary segmentation networks and a pretrained VGG16 used for feature loss during training
Description: OCT2HE is a CycleGAN-based image translation model that maps unpaired in vivo OCT images to H&E-like histology images while preserving skin layer boundaries and nuclei morphology.
Evaluation: Virtual staining; morphology preservation; boundary localization (SC, DEJ); nuclei consistency

Tool Usage: OCT2Hist: Non-Invasive Virtual Biopsy Using Optical Coherence Tomography
Description: OCT2Hist uses GAN architecture for OCT to histology image translation. CNN-based generator and discriminator. (2023)
Evaluation: Histopathology classification; Image translation quality (FID, SSIM)

Tool Usage: Noninvasive virtual biopsy by translating in vivo OCT images into virtual H&E histology using paired, micro-registered data
Architecture: Conditional GAN (pix2pix): ResNet encoder decoder generator + PatchGAN discriminator, trained on micro-registered OCT H&E pairs
Description: A conditional GAN trained on micron-accurately registered OCT-H&E pairs to generate H&E-like histology images from noninvasive OCT scans for virtual biopsy.
Evaluation: Virtual staining; morphology fidelity; clinical structure detection (e.g., hair follicles); qualitative and reader-study evaluation

Tool Usage: Learns PET-specific embeddings via self-supervised contrastive learning and diffusion priors.
Description: ALL-PET uses hybrid CNN/Transformer architecture for PET imaging foundation model. Combines convolutional and attention mechanisms. (2024)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: A suite of task-agnostic models. It provides frozen feature representations that generalized to new tasks without intensive fine-tuning.
Description: Scalable pre-training on 105k volumes using 3D adaptations of DINOv2 and depth-aware embeddings.
Evaluation: Low-shot transfer (AUROC); Feature test-retest; Segmentation; Retrieval.

Tool Usage: Natural language3DSAM is a multimodal foundation model that uses PET and other 3D medical images to generate Natural language-aligned embeddings for prompt-based segmentation via a SAM-inspired architecture.
Description: Natural language3DSAM uses ViT encoder + text encoder with SAM-based 3D decoder for language-guided medical image segmentation. (2024)
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: Ankh is a general-purpose protein language model that learns representations of amino acids to facilitate various downstream protein modeling tasks, including structure prediction, function prediction, and protein variant generation.
Architecture: Encoder-decoder transformer (T5-like) backbone. Pretrained using a self-supervised masked language modeling objective (1-gram span masking with 20% probability) on the UniRef50 dataset. Features relative positional embeddings and Gated-GELU activation.
Description: Ankh is an optimized, general-purpose protein language model trained on Google's TPU-v4. It achieves state-of-the-art performance on various structure and function benchmarks with significantly fewer parameters than existing models, emphasizing data-efficient and knowledge-guided optimization over sheer scale.
Evaluation: Evaluated on Protein Function Prediction (Fluorescence, Solubility, GB1 Fitness), Protein Structure Prediction (Contact Prediction, Fold Prediction, Secondary Structure Prediction, Embedding-based Annotation Transfer), Protein Localization Prediction, and Novel Protein Generation. Metrics include Accuracy, Spearman Correlation, L/1 and L/5 precision, RMSD, and Shannon Entropy. Benchmarks include CASP12, CASP14, ProteinNet, TAPE, FLIP, DeepSol, DeepLoc, and CATH. Efficiency was evaluated via feature extraction time and parameter count vs. performance trade-offs.

Tool Usage: ESM3 is a multimodal generative language model that reasons over the sequence, structure, and function of proteins to enable controllable generation of novel, functional proteins.
Architecture: Bidirectional transformer trained with a generative masked language modeling objective across discrete tokens representing protein sequence, 3D structure, and function. It uses a discrete autoencoder for structure tokenization and geometric attention for atomic coordinates.
Description: ESM3 is a frontier multimodal generative language model trained on 2.78 billion natural proteins. It can follow complex prompts combining its modalities to generate highly diverged functional proteins, such as a novel fluorescent protein (esmGFP) estimated to be equivalent to 500 million years of evolution.
Evaluation: Evaluated on structure prediction confidence (pTM, pLDDT), prompt consistency (backbone cRMSD, SS3 accuracy, SASA Spearman correlation, keyword recovery), structural similarity (TM-score), sequence identity, and CAMEO test set (LDDT). Experimental validation included synthesis, expression in E. coli, and measurement of fluorescence activity, brightness, and excitation/emission spectra.

Tool Usage: ProGen2 is a suite of autoregressive protein language models scaled up to 6.4 billion parameters that captures the distribution of evolutionary sequences to generate novel viable protein sequences and predict protein fitness without additional finetuning.
Architecture: Autoregressive transformer decoder with left-to-right causal masking, rotary positional encodings, and parallelized self-attention and feed-forward circuits. Pretrained via next-token prediction on over a billion protein sequences from genomic, metagenomic (Uniref90, BFD30), and immune repertoire (OAS) databases.
Description: ProGen2 is a family of large-scale protein language models ranging from 151M to 6.4B parameters, trained on diverse sequence datasets to explore the boundaries of sequence generation and zero-shot fitness prediction in protein engineering.
Evaluation: Tasks: Zero-shot fitness prediction, universal protein generation, fold-specific generation, antibody generation. Metrics: Perplexity (test-max90, test-max50), TMscore, sequence identity, pLDDT, Spearman correlation (rho), AUC, SAP score (aggregation propensity), CamSol-intrinsic profile (solubility). Benchmarks: Deep mutational scanning (DMS) datasets (narrow and wide landscapes including AAV, GFP, CM, GB1), and antibody-specific landscapes.

Tool Usage: ProtGPT2 is an autoregressive language model that generates de novo protein sequences following the principles of natural proteins. It is designed to efficiently produce fit, stable proteins that can sample unexplored regions of the protein space for high-throughput protein engineering.
Architecture: Decoder-only Transformer architecture (36 layers, 738 million parameters, 1280 dimensionality) based on GPT2-large. It was pretrained using an autoregressive (self-supervised) next-token prediction strategy on approximately 50 million unannotated protein sequences from the UniRef50 database, utilizing a Byte Pair Encoding (BPE) tokenizer.
Description: ProtGPT2 is a deep unsupervised language model trained on the entire protein space to generate novel protein sequences. It leverages a GPT-2-like architecture to learn the 'language' of proteins, enabling the zero-shot generation of complex, non-idealized globular protein structures that are distantly related to natural ones.
Evaluation: Evaluations include statistical sampling of amino acid propensities, disorder and secondary structure predictions (IUPred3, PSIPRED), remote homology detection (HHblits against Uniclust30), structural order and stability predictions (AlphaFold2 pLDDT scores, Rosetta RelaxBB energy scores), molecular dynamics simulations (RMSD flexibility patterns), and structural space network analysis (Protlego, FoldSeek).

Tool Usage: ProtTrans provides a suite of pre-trained protein language models that extract biophysical and structural features from single protein sequences to predict secondary structure, subcellular localization, and membrane solubility without relying on computationally expensive multiple sequence alignments.
Architecture: A suite of Transformer-based language models (including BERT, Albert, Electra, Transformer-XL, XLNet, and T5) pre-trained on massive protein sequence datasets (UniRef50, UniRef100, BFD) containing up to 393 billion amino acids using self-supervised learning (auto-encoding and auto-regressive objectives).
Description: ProtTrans explores the limits of up-scaling language models trained on proteins. It compares auto-regressive and auto-encoding pre-training effects on subsequent supervised training, demonstrating that embeddings from these models (especially ProtT5) can match or exceed state-of-the-art methods that traditionally rely on evolutionary information.
Evaluation: Evaluated on per-residue secondary structure prediction (Q3/Q8 accuracy on CASP12, TS115, CB513, and NEW364 datasets), per-protein subcellular localization (10-state accuracy Q10 on DeepLoc), and membrane vs. water-soluble classification (2-state accuracy Q2). Also evaluated for inference speed, hardware scaling efficiency, and t-SNE clustering of biophysical properties.

Tool Usage: ESM-1b is a deep contextual language model that learns representations from unannotated protein sequences to predict biological properties such as secondary structure, long-range contacts, and mutational effects.
Architecture: Deep Transformer (up to 33 layers, ~650M parameters) trained using a masked language modeling (MLM) self-supervised objective on amino acid character sequences from the UniParc and UniRef databases.
Description: The model applies self-supervised learning to scale a deep contextual language model across evolutionary diversity (250 million sequences). It demonstrates that biological structure and function, from biochemical properties to remote homology, emerge in the learned representations without prior knowledge or explicit structural supervision.
Evaluation: Language modeling fidelity (Exponentiated Cross Entropy); Remote homology detection (Hit-10, AUC on SCOPe); Secondary structure prediction (8-class accuracy on CB513, CASP13); Long-range contact prediction (Top-L precision on RaptorX, CASP11, CASP12, CASP13); Mutational effect prediction (Spearman correlation on Envision and DeepSequence datasets).

Tool Usage: 650M transformer using ESM2 protein embeddings across 36M cells from 8 species
Description: Universal Cell Embeddings (UCE) uses Transformer encoder architecture for learning universal cell representations across species and tissues. (Rosen et al., Nature Methods 2024)
Evaluation: Reverse complement equivariance; Long-range genomic benchmarks; Variant effect prediction

Tool Usage: 100M parameter model covering ~20K genes from 50M+ human single cells
Description: scFoundation adopts an asymmetric encoder-decoder architecture. The encoder only accepts embeddings of non-zero and non-masked expressed genes; the decoder accepts all genes' embeddings. This asymmetric transformer-like architecture enables efficient learning. Also known as xTrimoGene. (Hao et al., Nature Methods 2024)
Evaluation: Cell embedding quality; Trajectory inference; Perturbation response; Cross-tissue generalization

Tool Usage: BERT-based model using gene2vec embeddings for cell type annotation tasks
Description: scBERT is the first single-cell pre-training model constructed based on the BERT architecture. Employs Performer for efficient attention with gene2vec embeddings. Uses bidirectional encoder with masked gene prediction. (Yang et al., Nature Machine Intelligence 2022)
Evaluation: Cell type annotation; Marker gene identification; Batch correction evaluation

Tool Usage: A specialized foundation model continually pretrained on scGPT (scRNA-seq model) to harmonize various spatial technologies and support cell-type deconvolution and missing gene imputation.
Description: scGPT-spatial adapts scGPT's GPT-style decoder architecture for spatial transcriptomics data. Autoregressive Transformer with spatial context. (2024)
Evaluation: Cell type annotation; Gene perturbation; Batch integration; Multi-omics integration; 33M cell benchmark

Tool Usage: LPFM is a unified low-level pathology foundation model that enhances image quality in restoration tasks (super-resolution, deblurring, denoising) via natural language prompt guidance
Architecture: Two-stage framework using a contrastive pre-trained KL-Autoencoder for stain-invariant features and a conditional Latent Diffusion Model (U-Net) guided by a CLIP text encoder.
Description: LPFM utilizes a multi-source dataset of 190 million patches to establish a unified generative framework for image restoration, relying on textual prompts to dynamically switch between low-level tasks
Evaluation: Evaluated on 66 tasks including super-resolution, deblurring, and denoising using metrics like PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS, MAE, and Pearson correlation on datasets like CAMELYON16, PAIP2020, and PANDA

Tool Usage: AIDO.Tissue is a spatially informed foundation model that jointly encodes multiple neighboring cells using an asymmetric encoder-decoder architecture to capture cross-cell dependencies at scale, enabling accurate tissue-level tasks such as spatial cell-type classification, niche prediction, and cell density estimation.
Description: AIDO.Tissue uses Transformer encoder for tissue-level representation learning across pathology modalities. (ModelScope, 2024)
Evaluation: Multi-task benchmark evaluation

Tool Usage: SToFM is a multi-scale spatial transcriptomics foundation model that learns cell representations by integrating tissue-, neighborhood-, and gene-level information using an SE(2) Transformer, enabling large-scale pretraining on high-resolution ST data and strong performance on tasks such as tissue region segmentation and cell-type annotation.
Description: SToFM (Spatial Transcriptomics Foundation Model) uses Transformer encoder for learning spatial gene expression patterns. (2024)
Evaluation: Multi-task benchmark evaluation

Tool Usage: stFormer is a transformer-based foundation model specifically designed for spatial transcriptomics, using cross-attention to integrate spatial ligand information and unify heterogeneous ST technologies, enabling improved clustering, batch correction, cell-type and gene-function prediction, and inference of ligand _ Ã¶ _ ‘â _ receptor signaling via in silico perturbation.
Description: stFomer uses Transformer encoder architecture for spatial transcriptomics analysis with attention over spatial neighborhoods. (2024)
Evaluation: Multi-task benchmark evaluation

Tool Usage: Novae is a graph-based foundation model used to learn spatially aware cell representations from large-scale spatial transcriptomics data, enabling zero-shot spatial domain inference, batch-effect correction, hierarchical domain construction, and multiple downstream spatial analyses across tissues, gene panels, and technologies.
Description: Novae is a graph-based foundation model using Graph Neural Network combined with attention mechanisms for spatial transcriptomics. Hybrid GNN + Attention architecture. (Nature Methods 2025)
Evaluation: Multi-task benchmark evaluation

Tool Usage: Foundation model for spatial and dissociated transcriptomics on 110M cells
Description: Nicheformer uses Transformer encoder architecture with niche-aware attention for spatial transcriptomics, modeling cellular neighborhoods. (2024)
Evaluation: Multi-task benchmark evaluation

Tool Usage: This is a "Generalist" medical foundation model trained on 3.3 million images across 7 modalities, including both Pathology patches (H&E) and TBP.
Architecture: Dual-network teacher-student ViT framework with a dynamic memory module
Description: A multimodal, multi-specialty foundation model trained using self-supervised learning (SSL) to unify the interpretation of diverse imaging types through a shared latent space.
Evaluation: Identification of skin lesions and diagnosis of dermatological diseases from clinical and dermoscopic images.

Tool Usage: Unified dermatology foundation model pretrained on large-scale total body photography (TBP) tiles for skin phenotype assessment, melanoma screening, risk stratification, and malignant lesion detection across patient-level workflows.
Architecture: ViT-Large Image Encoder + Masked Latent Regressor
Description: PanDerm learns robust TBP representations via masked latent modeling and CLIP-based semantic alignment, enabling large-scale melanoma screening, nevus counting, photodamage assessment, and patient-level risk prediction using whole-body images.
Evaluation: Melanoma screening, risk stratification, phenotype quantification

Tool Usage: Think of this as an "Auto-Highlighter." It s a tool that can look at a high-resolution photo of your entire body and instantly find and circle every single mole, no matter how small or oddly shaped.
Description: Skin-SAM adapts SAM encoder-decoder for skin lesion segmentation.
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: Classification, segmentation, and object detection
Architecture: ResNet-50 or ViT-B/16 backbones with a second-order graph matching SSL formulation
Description: A general-purpose medical vision foundation model pre-trained on 1.3 million multi-modal images using graph-based structural constraints.
Evaluation: Skin lesion segmentation and melanoma classification.

Tool Usage: Interactive system for dermatological diagnoses
Description: SkinGPT-4 uses ViT + LLM for dermatology diagnosis
Evaluation: 150 real dermatology cases

Tool Usage: This is a "Generalist" medical foundation model trained on 3.3 million images across 7 modalities, including both Pathology patches (H&E) and TBP.
Architecture: Dual-network teacher-student ViT framework with a dynamic memory module
Description: A multimodal, multi-specialty foundation model trained using self-supervised learning (SSL) to unify the interpretation of diverse imaging types through a shared latent space.
Evaluation: Identification of breast cancer on ultrasound scans.

Tool Usage: Federated learning ultrasound foundation model across 16 institutions (vision-only)
Description: ViT encoder with federated learning for ultrasound.
Evaluation: Multi-task benchmark evaluation

Tool Usage: Universal ultrasound foundation model pretrained via self-supervised MIM on 2M+ images across 12 organs (vision-only)
Description: USFM uses ViT encoder architecture as an ultrasound foundation model. Self-supervised learning on diverse ultrasound imaging. (2024)
Evaluation: Multi-task benchmark evaluation

Tool Usage: Classification, segmentation, and object detection
Architecture: ResNet-50 or ViT-B/16 backbones with a second-order graph matching SSL formulation
Description: A general-purpose medical vision foundation model pre-trained on 1.3 million multi-modal images using graph-based structural constraints.
Evaluation: Breast cancer segmentation and fetal head circumference measurement.

Tool Usage: Domain-specific foundation model for fetal ultrasound classification using DINOv2 self-supervision.
Description: UltraDINO uses ViT encoder with DINO self-supervised learning adapted for ultrasound imaging. Encoder-only design. (2024)
Evaluation: Linear probe evaluation; Fine-tuning benchmark

Tool Usage: A specialized generalist foundation model for ophthalmology, pre-trained on 11 ophthalmic modalities including B-scan Ultrasound and Color Fundus Photography (CFP). It learns robust cross-modal representations, enabling efficient adaptation for various downstream ophthalmic tasks.
Description: EyeFound uses Vision Transformer encoder as a foundation model for comprehensive ophthalmic imaging. Encoder-only ViT design. (2024)
Evaluation: 11 ophthalmic modalities; Cross-modal evaluation; Disease screening; Prognosis prediction

Tool Usage: Another powerful ophthalmic generalist foundation model, pre-trained on 3.4 million images across 8 ophthalmic modalities (including CFP and B-scan Ultrasound). It demonstrates strong generalization across disease screening, prognosis, and segmentation, even for modalities not explicitly seen during pre-training.
Description: VisionFM uses ViT encoder as a general medical vision foundation model. Encoder-only architecture with self-supervised learning. (2024)
Evaluation: 8 ophthalmic modalities; Disease screening; Prognosis; Segmentation; Systemic biomarker prediction

Tool Usage: A specialized foundation model for echocardiography (cardiac ultrasound), which utilizes synchronously recorded ECG signals as a supervisory signal through periodic contrastive learning to capture the dynamic features of heart motion. This explicitly links the two modalities for enhanced cardiac analysis.
Description: EchoFM uses Vision Transformer encoder as a foundation model for echocardiography. Self-supervised pretraining on large echo datasets. (2024)
Evaluation: ECG-guided cardiac analysis; Periodic contrastive learning; Motion feature capture

Tool Usage: Generates MR-like fetal brain images from clinical ultrasound to improve interpretability for non-experts and enable downstream tasks such as automatic US  MRI registration and multimodal fusion when paired data are unavailable.
Description: Dual encoder for ultrasound-MRI multimodal learning.
Evaluation: Radiology image classification

Tool Usage: This is a "Generalist" medical foundation model trained on 3.3 million images across 7 modalities, including both Pathology patches (H&E) and TBP.
Architecture: Dual-network teacher-student ViT framework with a dynamic memory module
Description: A multimodal, multi-specialty foundation model trained using self-supervised learning (SSL) to unify the interpretation of diverse imaging types through a shared latent space.
Evaluation: Diagnosis of pneumonia, tuberculosis, and pneumothorax on chest X-rays (CXR).

Tool Usage: A multi-anatomy X-ray foundation model for 20 downstream tasks, including classification, retrieval, segmentation, localization, visual grounding, and report generation.
Architecture: ViT-B vision encoder (DINOv2) linked via MLP to a LLaMA-7B language decoder
Description: A multi-anatomy foundation model trained on 1.15 million images from head to toe to generalize across diverse clinical tasks in radiology.
Evaluation: Multi-anatomy radiographic interpretation, including chest pathologies, musculoskeletal abnormalities, bone fractures, and clinical report generation.

Tool Usage: A fully open AI foundation model that accrues heterogeneous knowledge for chest X-ray diagnosis.
Architecture: Swin transformer large encoder + projector + multi-task heads
Description: A fully open AI foundation model for chest radiography that accrues knowledge from heterogeneous expert labels across diverse datasets to improve diagnostic scope and generalizability
Evaluation: Common thoracic diseases (e.g., atelectasis, cardiomegaly), rare conditions (e.g., pneumoperitoneum), COVID-19 screening, and sex-related bias tolerance.

Tool Usage: Classification, segmentation, and object detection
Architecture: ResNet-50 or ViT-B/16 backbones with a second-order graph matching SSL formulation
Description: A general-purpose medical vision foundation model pre-trained on 1.3 million multi-modal images using graph-based structural constraints.
Evaluation: Lung disease detection, chest multi-organ segmentation, and dental panoramic analysis.

Tool Usage: Delphi-2M is a generative pretrained transformer model that predicts the rates and timings of over 1,000 diseases based on an individual's past health history, enabling the sampling of synthetic future health trajectories.
Architecture: Modified GPT-2 architecture with continuous age encoding (using sine/cosine basis functions), causal continuous time masks, and a dual-head output for predicting the next disease token (softmax) and the time to the next event (exponential waiting time model).
Description: Delphi-2M is a generative transformer trained on 0.4 million UK Biobank participants to model the natural history of human disease. It processes sequences of ICD-10 diagnostic codes and lifestyle factors to predict future multi-disease incidences and generate synthetic health trajectories.
Evaluation: Evaluated on UK Biobank (longitudinal testing) and Danish disease registries (external validation). Metrics include age- and sex-stratified AUROC, cross-entropy loss, and calibration curves. Compared against standard epidemiological baselines, clinical risk scores (QRisk3, Score2, Framingham, Charlson, Elixhauser), and machine learning models (AutoPrognosis, LLama3.1, MILTON). Explainability assessed via SHAP values and UMAP embeddings.

Tool Usage: Lingshu is a medical-specialized Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) designed for unified multimodal medical understanding and reasoning, capable of performing tasks such as multimodal question answering, text-based QA, and medical report generation.
Architecture: Built upon the Qwen2.5-VL model architecture (7B and 32B parameters), consisting of a vision encoder, an MLP-based projector, and a large language model. It is trained using a four-stage paradigm: Medical Shallow Alignment, Medical Deep Alignment, Medical Instruction Tuning, and Medical-oriented Reinforcement Learning (RLVR via GRPO).
Description: A generalist foundation model tailored for the medical domain, trained on a meticulously curated dataset of 3.75M open-source and 1.30M synthetic medical samples. It leverages multi-stage training and reinforcement learning to enhance medical reasoning and multimodal understanding across diverse clinical scenarios.
Evaluation: Evaluated using the MedEvalKit framework across 16 benchmarks spanning multimodal QA (VQA-RAD, SLAKE, PathVQA, PMC-VQA, OmniMedVQA, MMMU-Med, MedXpertQA), text-only QA (MMLU, PubMedQA, MedMCQA, MedQA-USMLE, MedBullets, SuperGPQA), and report generation (MIMIC-CXR, IU-Xray, CheXpert Plus). Metrics include Accuracy, ROUGE-L, CIDEr, RaTEScore, SembScore, and RadCliQ-v1.

Tool Usage: MedVersa is a generalist foundation model that accepts heterogeneous inputs, including medical images and clinical text, to generate flexible outputs such as classifications, segmentations, and narrative reports across diverse imaging workflows.
Architecture: The architecture consists of a multimodal input coordinator with a 2D Swin Transformer encoder and a 3D U-Net encoder, vision-language adapters, and a Llama-2-Chat large language model acting as an optimizable orchestrator. It also includes specialized vision modules (a lightweight detection module, 2D U-Net, and 3D U-Net) for vision-centric tasks, trained using low-rank adaptation (LoRA).
Description: MedVersa is a generalist medical artificial intelligence (GMAI) system trained on 29 million instances across 91 datasets. It uses an LLM to analyze visual and linguistic inputs and decide whether to generate language outputs directly or route tasks to specialized vision modules for detection and segmentation.
Evaluation: Evaluated on nine medical imaging tasks including image captioning, longitudinal captioning, region captioning, open-ended VQA, image classification, object detection, and 2D/3D image segmentation. Metrics include BLEU-4, BERTScore, CheXbert, RadGraph, RadCliQ, F1 score, mean IoU, and mean Dice score. Clinical evaluation included blinded radiologist assessments of report clinical equivalence, discrepancy rates, and report-writing time efficiency.

Tool Usage: MedVersa is a generalist foundation model that accepts heterogeneous inputs, including medical images and clinical text, to generate flexible outputs such as classifications, segmentations, and narrative reports across diverse imaging workflows.
Architecture: The architecture consists of a multimodal input coordinator with a 2D Swin Transformer encoder and a 3D U-Net encoder, vision-language adapters, and a Llama-2-Chat large language model acting as an optimizable orchestrator. It also includes specialized vision modules (a lightweight detection module, 2D U-Net, and 3D U-Net) for vision-centric tasks, trained using low-rank adaptation (LoRA).
Description: MedVersa is a generalist medical artificial intelligence (GMAI) system trained on 29 million instances across 91 datasets. It uses an LLM to analyze visual and linguistic inputs and decide whether to generate language outputs directly or route tasks to specialized vision modules for detection and segmentation.
Evaluation: Evaluated on nine medical imaging tasks including image captioning, longitudinal captioning, region captioning, open-ended VQA, image classification, object detection, and 2D/3D image segmentation. Metrics include BLEU-4, BERTScore, CheXbert, RadGraph, RadCliQ, F1 score, mean IoU, and mean Dice score. Clinical evaluation included blinded radiologist assessments of report clinical equivalence, discrepancy rates, and report-writing time efficiency.

Tool Usage: mSTAR is a multimodal pathology foundation model that integrates whole-slide images, pathology reports, and gene expression data to inject whole-slide context into patch representations for various oncological tasks.
Architecture: Two-stage pretraining framework: Stage 1 pretrains a slide aggregator (TransMIL for WSIs, BioBERT for text, Performer/scBERT for RNA-Seq) using inter-modality and inter-cancer contrastive learning. Stage 2 uses self-taught training to propagate the aggregator's multimodal knowledge into a patch extractor (ViT-L) via a siamese network with exponential moving average (EMA).
Description: mSTAR (Multimodal Self-TAught PRetraining) is a pathology foundation model pretrained on over 116 million patch images from 26,169 slide-level modality pairs across 32 cancer types. It bridges patch-level and slide-level contexts by aligning H&E slides, pathology reports, and bulk RNA-Seq data.
Evaluation: Evaluated on 97 tasks across 15 application types (7 categories: Pathological Diagnosis, Molecular Prediction, Report Generation, Survival Prediction, Multimodal Fusion, Zero-shot Slide Classification, Zero-shot Slide Retrieval). Metrics include Macro-AUC, C-Index, Recall@5/10/50, BLEU, METEOR, and ROUGE-L.

Tool Usage: TITAN is a multimodal whole-slide foundation model that extracts general-purpose slide representations and generates pathology reports. It enables zero-shot classification, cross-modal retrieval, and rare disease retrieval without requiring task-specific fine-tuning.
Architecture: Backbone: Vision Transformer (ViT) slide encoder with ALiBi positional encoding, paired with a text encoder and multimodal text decoder. Pretraining strategy: Three-stage process including (1) vision-only self-supervised learning via iBOT on 2D feature grids extracted by CONCHv1.5, (2) vision-language alignment using CoCa with synthetic ROI captions, and (3) vision-language alignment with WSI-level pathology reports. Modal-specific design: Employs 2D ALiBi positional encoding to handle long and variable input sequences of patch features while preserving spatial context.
Description: TITAN (Transformer-based pathology Image and Text Alignment Network) is a multimodal foundation model pretrained on 335,645 whole-slide images and aligned with 423,122 synthetic captions and 182,862 pathology reports to capture both fine-grained and global histomorphological semantics.
Evaluation: Tasks: Morphological subtyping, molecular classification, survival prediction, rare cancer retrieval, cross-modal retrieval, zero-shot/few-shot classification, and report generation. Metrics: Balanced accuracy, AUROC, c-index, ECE, Accuracy@K, MVAcc@K, Recall@K, METEOR, ROUGE, and BLEU. Benchmarks: TCGA, EBRAINS, PANDA, BRACS, and multiple internal/external cohorts (e.g., Rare-Cancer-External). Efficiency/Scaling: Ablations conducted on pretraining data scale (12.5% to 100%), positional encodings, and network depth.
